Professor Dehuan Liu Department: School of New Media of Peking University Title: Professor Position: Vice Dean Resume Dehuan Liu Title: Professor Degree: Doctor ## Resume: 1985- 1989 Bachelor of Sociology, Sociology Department of Peking University; 1989- 1992 Master of Sociology, Sociology Department of Peking University;

text_blocks.text_content: 1999- 2001 Ph.D. of Sociology, Sociology Department of Peking University.
1992- 2001 Lecturer of Sociology Department of Peking University
2002- 2007 Associate Professor of School of Journalism & Communication of Peking University;
2007- 2014 Professor of School of Journalism & Communication of Peking University
Feb. 2014 to the present Professor and Vice Dean of School of New Media of Peking University

Currently serving as the of School of New Media of Peking University, President of China Marketing Research Association, Vice President of China Association Of Market Information and Research, Director of Market and Media Research Center of Peking University, and a doctoral supervisor.
## Exchange Visit:
From Jul. to Sep. 1995, he participated in the "First Advanced Statistics Training Course" organized by the Ford Foundation at Peking University;
From Feb. to Aug. 1995, he participated in the ESEC English training for Peking University teachers held by America at Peking University;
From Jan. to Apr. 1996, he participated in the "Young Sociologists Training Program" in Department of Sociology, The Chinese University of Hong Kong;
From Jul. to Sep. 1997, he participated in the "Second Advanced Statistics Training Course" organized by the Ford Foundation at Chinese Academy of Social Sciences;
In Dec. 2000, he participated in the "Symposium in Memory of Qing Yang" held at The Chinese University of Hong Kong and announced the academic paper "Ambiguous Social Basis of Sexual Harassment".
From Feb. 2005 to Jan. 2006, he served as a visiting scholar at Harvard University.
## Monographs:
1. Marketing Research, Economy & Management Publishing House, 2000
2. Theoretical Basis and Practical Methods of Social Survey, co-author, China

1999- 2001 Ph.D. of Sociology, Sociology Department of Peking University.  

1992- 2001 Lecturer of Sociology Department of Peking University  

2002- 2007 Associate Professor of School of Journalism & Communication of Peking University;  

2007- 2014 Professor of School of Journalism & Communication of Peking University  

Feb. 2014 to the present Professor and Vice Dean of School of New Media of Peking University  

Currently serving as the of School of New Media of Peking University, President of China Marketing Research Association, Vice President of China Association Of Market Information and Research, Director of Market and Media Research Center of Peking University, and a doctoral supervisor.  

## Exchange Visit:  

From Jul. to Sep. 1995, he participated in the "First Advanced Statistics Training Course" organized by the Ford Foundation at Peking University;  

From Feb. to Aug. 1995, he participated in the ESEC English training for Peking University teachers held by America at Peking University;  

From Jan. to Apr. 1996, he participated in the "Young Sociologists Training Program" in Department of Sociology, The Chinese University of Hong Kong;  

From Jul. to Sep. 1997, he participated in the "Second Advanced Statistics Training Course" organized by the Ford Foundation at Chinese Academy of Social Sciences;  

In Dec. 2000, he participated in the "Symposium in Memory of Qing Yang" held at The Chinese University of Hong Kong and announced the academic paper "Ambiguous Social Basis of Sexual Harassment".  

From Feb. 2005 to Jan. 2006, he served as a visiting scholar at Harvard University.  

## Monographs:  

1. Marketing Research, Economy & Management Publishing House, 2000  

2. Theoretical Basis and Practical Methods of Social Survey, co-author, China

text_blocks.text_content: ## Scientific Research Projects:

1. In 2000, he participated in the "Research on Social Network and Mental Health" (in cooperation with The Chinese University of Hong Kong); 2. From 1999 to 2000, he presided over the "Research on World Bank Beijing Environment Project" (in cooperation with the World Bank); 3. In 1998, he presided over the "(Lifestyle), (China Business News) series of research"; 4. In 1997, he participated in "Research on Social Network"; 5. In 1997, he presided over the "Hope Project Evaluation Project" (in cooperation with China Youth Development Foundation); 6. In 1997, he presided over the "Research on Chinese Reading Life of Twenty Years"; 7. In 1997, he participated in the "Research on Higher Income Community Lifestyle"; 8. From 1992 to 1996, he participated in the "Research on District Economy and Community Development" (in cooperation with Shenhe District Government, Shenyang City); 9. From 1996 to 1998, he participated in the "Research on Migrant Workers"; 10. In 1998, he presided over the "Credit Research"; 12. In 2000, he participated in the "Research on Migrant Population" (in cooperation with America); 13. In 1996, he participated in the "Research on Chinese Entrepreneur" (in cooperation with EU); 14. From 1992 to 1993, he presided over the "Research on Chinese Wholesale Market" (in cooperation with Shenhe District Government, Shenyang City); 15. In 2000, he presided over the "Research on Post and Telecommunication System" (in cooperation with Beijing International Post Office); 16. In 2001, he continued to presided over the "Research on World Bank Beijing Environment Project" (in cooperation with the World Bank). 17. In 2001, he presided over the "Research on Beijing Newspaper Retail Sale Market"; 18. In 2001, he participated in the "Research on the Culture Development Strategy of China Life";

## Honors and Awards:
In 2007, he was selected as “Top Ten Teachers of Peking University”
In 2008, he was selected as a scholar of Beijing “Hundred Talents Project”

His book Age Theory: Social Time in Social Space won the third prize of the Award for Outstanding Achievements in Scientific Research of Higher Education Institutions (Humanities and Social Sciences).
## Research Areas
Market and media analysis, communication research methods, Internet and mobile Internet research, data analysis and data mining, market research, etc.
